symptom: Photo shows a Federal Pacific Stab-Lok residential panel or breakers. The electrician needs to identify the panel and decide whether to proceed.
diagnosis: Legacy Federal Pacific Stab-Lok panel. Treat as high risk because breakers have a known failure-to-trip history and replacement parts do not remove the panel-level concern.
procedure: Identify the deadfront label and breaker type before touching conductors.
Kill power upstream if work requires removing breakers or landing conductors.
Verify de-energized with a meter on line and load conductors.
Document the panel make, visible defects, and any double taps or overheating.
Recommend evaluation or replacement instead of expanding the panel.
safety_notes: Do not rely on the branch breaker alone for worker protection.
Do not add load or new circuits to a known problem panel.
If bus damage, heat discoloration, or loose breakers are visible, stop and escalate.
